label: Uneasy calm
summary: A day in waiting. The U.S.-Iran memorandum to end their three-month war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz was now signed - Trump and President Pezeshkian inked it electronically as Trump dined with Macron at Versailles on June 17 - with negotiating teams due in Geneva on Friday, June 19 and tankers already exiting the lifting U.S. blockade. But the deal sat under strain: Israel, left out of it, kept striking southern Lebanon, wounding five of its own soldiers to Hezbollah drones near Nabatieh, and Tehran warned the continued strikes and occupation would breach the accord, claiming Israel violated the ceasefire 84 times in two days. Lebanon counts over 3,800 dead and a million displaced since March. The economic mood cooled: in Kevin Warsh's first decision the Federal Reserve held at 3.50-3.75% but its dot plot dropped the prospect of a cut and nine of nineteen members now see a hike by year-end, with inflation at a multi-year high of 4.2% on the oil shock; Wall Street fell on the hawkish turn - the Dow off 507 points, the S&P down 1.21%, the Nasdaq 1.34% - and two-year yields jumped to a one-year high. The harder ledger held firm. NOAA declared an El Nino advisory, with forecasters putting strong-to-historic odds on the event by autumn, even as more than a third of World Cup matches faced dangerously humid heat. Sudan's IPC-confirmed famine in El Fasher and Kadugli ground on. In Haiti, a sliver of resolve against the gloom: UN chief Guterres visited Port-au-Prince to stand up a new gang-suppression force, against 2,300 killed and 1.5 million displaced this year. Science kept its steady pace - a fully computer-designed vaccine cleared a first human trial, base editing reached early human embryos. A war signed but not yet sealed, a hawkish Fed and a hardening climate signal hold the mood at the soft, uneasy middle.

drivers.headline: The U.S.-Iran memorandum to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is signed - Trump and President Pezeshkian ink it electronically as Trump dines with Macron at Versailles - with negotiating teams set to gather in Geneva on Friday, June 19 and tankers already exiting the lifting U.S. blockade

Israeli air strikes on Lebanon continue despite the U.S.-Iran deal - drone attacks in Tyre and raids near Nabatieh wound five Israeli soldiers to Hezbollah drones - as Tehran warns the strikes and continued occupation breach the accord, claiming 84 ceasefire violations in two days

The Federal Reserve holds at 3.50-3.75% in Chair Kevin Warsh's first decision but drops its dot-plot outlook for a cut - nine of nineteen members now see a hike by year-end - with May inflation at a multi-year-high 4.2% on the oil shock

Wall Street falls on the Fed's hawkish turn: the Dow drops 507 points (0.98%), the S&P 500 1.21% and the Nasdaq 1.34%, while two-year Treasury yields jump 16 basis points to 4.21%, their highest in over a year

NOAA declares an El Nino advisory, with forecasters putting an ~88% chance on at least a strong event and a ~63% chance of a 'very strong' one by autumn - a temporary global temperature boost on top of the long-term warming trend

More than a third of 2026 World Cup matches face dangerously hot, humid conditions, with Houston and Miami the highest-risk host cities, prompting FIFA cooling breaks, evening kickoffs and cooled benches as climate change raises the odds of extreme-heat play

A turning point for Haiti? UN Secretary-General Guterres visits Port-au-Prince to stand up a new Security Council-approved gang-suppression force, against UN figures of 2,300 killed and over 1.5 million displaced this year as gangs control up to 90% of the capital

Sudan's famine grinds on: with IPC-confirmed famine in besieged El Fasher and Kadugli, WFP has been unable to reach El Fasher by road for over a year, malnutrition screening runs 38-75%, and the 2026 humanitarian response plan is only about 16% funded

Swiss voters reject the right-wing Swiss People's Party initiative to cap the population at 10 million - nearly 55% against on a 59% turnout - averting a forced collision with the EU over free movement

Japan's PM Takaichi tours Britain and Italy before the G7, sealing cooperation on economic security, frontier technology, offshore wind and nuclear with the UK and on supply chains, semiconductors, critical minerals and space with Italy

The first human trial of a wholly computer-designed vaccine - Cambridge and DIOSynVax's pEVAC-PS - proves safe and triggers broad immune responses to SARS-CoV-2, SARS-CoV-1 and related bat coronaviruses, while researchers also report the first base editing of early human embryos

Good-news ledger: Monterey Park, California passes the country's first permanent ban on data centers by an 86% ballot margin, five Przewalski's horses are reintroduced to the Kazakh steppe, and China's EV surge is credited with preventing some 260,000 premature deaths from cleaner air

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (2026 in science / 2026 Iran war ceasefire / 2026 Lebanon war / 2026 Swiss referendums / 2026 Colombian presidential election)
